description: Figure 8 presents two calculated equilibrium phase-fraction diagrams plotting phase percentage versus temperature for Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels, with calculations performed using FactSage 6.4 software as stated in the caption. Subfigure (a) shows alloy Fe-13.5Mn-6.3Al-0.78C, where ferrite (α) dominates at low temperatures (~85% at 400–550°C) and progressively transforms to austenite (γ) reaching 100% above ~780°C. This alloy exhibits a κ-carbide peak near 8% around 600–650°C, minor M23C6 at lower temperatures, and transient M7C3 between ~550 and 650°C. Subfigure (b) depicts alloy Fe-18Mn-7Al-0.85C, displaying a broader κ-carbide stability window with a higher maximum fraction of ~13–14% and full γ-stabilization above ~750°C, alongside M23C6 at temperatures below ~500°C. The side-by-side comparison reveals that higher Mn and Al contents expand the austenite temperature window and promote greater κ-carbide precipitation, providing thermodynamic guidance for designing precipitation-strengthened or duplex lightweight steels.
